subject: Biology
title: Autoimmune Diseases
description: Learn Autoimmune Diseases in one minute.

hook.text: In autoimmune diseases, the immune system attacks the body's own healthy cells—friendly fire.

buildup.text: Type 1 diabetes occurs when immune cells destroy insulin-producing cells in the pancreas.

discovery.text: Rheumatoid arthritis attacks joints. Multiple sclerosis attacks nerve insulation. Lupus attacks many organs.

twist.text: Women are three times more likely to develop autoimmune diseases—and scientists still aren't sure why.

climax.text: Over 80 autoimmune diseases are known. Together, they affect about 5–8% of the population.

punchline.text: When the army turns on its own country.

quiz.question: What happens in an autoimmune disease?
options: The immune system attacks healthy body cells
The immune system shuts down completely
Pathogens disable all white blood cells
